steps.title: Find Bartley's claim
steps.content: Bartley claims that any misfortune in the world lands on him. The underlined comparison follows that claim and turns it into a concrete image.

steps.title: Treat the image as emphasis
steps.content: Choice A treats the phrase as an event Bartley saw. Choice B calls it a counterexample, and Choice D shifts focus to Mrs. Tarpey. The simile makes Bartley's complaint more vivid and forceful. The answer is C.
